summary: Excellent soundboard recording captures a stellar August ’72 Berkeley show with standout Truckin’ → The Other One, Black Peter and a scorching Friend of the Devil.
review: The performance is widely praised as one of the band’s finest of 1972 – tight, focused, and full of magical moments, especially the powerful Truckin' → The Other One transition, a blistering Friend of the Devil, and an immaculate Black Peter. The Betty (soundboard) source is described as pristine, balanced and crystal‑clear with only minor crackles or a quieter segment on the rare Frozen Logger track. Overall the show and its mix are regarded as top‑tier Dead listening.

ai_rating.rationale: Over twenty detailed reviews consistently praise both musical performance and soundboard recording quality; the show is described as exemplary with only minor quirks, yielding a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, full‑balanced mix with clear vocals and great stereo separation; a few tiny crackles and one noticeably quieter segment (Frozen Logger) but overall pristine and professional sounding.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as well‑rehearsed, composed yet intense, with a strong first half of darker material followed by an extended, flowing second half highlighted by the Truckin'/Other One jam and tight transitions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for top‑form guitar work and vocal presence – described as 'singing into my ear' and 'top form'.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for a lengthy bass solo on Truckin', an octave effect used throughout the show, and solid overall playing.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for cool, well‑mixed guitar work on Friend of the Devil; his guitar is singled out as a rare treat in the mix.
